title: Liquidity (M2) & Monetary Base, Central Bank of Iran Annual Review (FY1379-1401)
notes: Central Bank of Iran's own primary annual figures for Liquidity (M2, 23/23 years) and Monetary Base (growth rate 19/23 years, level 5/23 years), transcribed by a prior session (2026-07-13) from 23 CBI Annual Review PDFs recovered via Wayback Machine (cbi.ir itself geo-blocks direct access; the Wayback route worked). This is a genuinely primary-Iranian-government source (CBI is the issuer, not a re-hosted mirror) already fully processed with a real-USD/real-2015 computed variant and its own README, but was NEVER registered in CHART_REGISTRY.csv until this pass -- confirmed via grep before writing this row, zero prior hits. Registering it as 'new' rather than 'extends' because no other chart_id in the registry represents CBI's own M2/monetary-base measure at all (the closest relatives are the Pahlavi-era pre-1971 archival fragments, which this pass separately links via a distinct extends-row below on the IMF IFS money-supply series -- the CBI Annual Review data and the IMF IFS data are different metrics/sources spanning different eras, kept as two rows rather than force-merged). This chart alone turns what had been an invisible, orphaned processed file into the single longest continuous *directly-CBI-sourced* M2 series in the project (23 unbroken fiscal years).

title: Government Debt to the Central Bank of Iran, Quarterly (1357-1395 SH / 1978-2016)
notes: Quarterly total government debt to CBI, split into excl.-government-corporations and government-corporations-only sub-components, billion rials. Source: Iran Data Portal (Syracuse University), itself mirroring a Central Bank of Iran table (original filename 'Government-Debt-to-the-Central-Bank-1978-to-2016-Quarterly-1-1.xls'); downloaded by a prior session (2026-07-12) but never harmonized or registered until this pass. Government debt to the central bank is a standard direct proxy for the government-financing channel of monetary-base creation -- not M1/M2 itself, but the single best-documented, most-continuous monetary-adjacent Iranian series covering the 1971-2000 gap between the IMF IFS historical money-supply series (ends 1971, see the extends-row below) and the CBI Annual Review M2 series (starts FY1379/2000-01, chart_id iran_monetary__liquidity_m2_monetary_base_2000_2023 above). Registered as 'new' since no existing chart_id represents this specific quarterly government-debt-to-CBI measure at all. Genuinely rare for this era of Iran data: quarterly, not annual, and essentially gap-free across nearly 40 years spanning the revolution, the Iran-Iraq War, and the post-war reconstruction era.

title: Money Supply (M1), Quasi-Money, Reserve Money & Domestic Credit, IMF IFS Historical (1937-1971)
notes: Money-supply/banking/national-accounts subset (22 of 54 metrics; trade/price-index/FX metrics from the same source excluded as out-of-cluster) of a prior session's hand-transcription of 15 IMF International Financial Statistics monthly-issue PDFs -- itself downloaded and transcribed 2026-07-13 (data/raw/imf-ifs-historical/) but never harmonized into data/processed/ nor registered in CHART_REGISTRY.csv until this pass. Marked 'extends' onto pahlavi__money_supply_changes_1957_59 (currently a thin 1957-59 snapshot from a single 1960 World Bank report table) because both represent the same core measure (Iran's money supply level) and this new series turns that 3-year snapshot into a 35-year span (1937-1971) with 21 annual M1 points plus quasi-money/reserve-money/domestic-credit/claims-on-government components that did not exist in the registry under any chart_id before. Also directly overlaps and cross-validates against pahlavi__monetary_survey_1963_70 and pahlavi__bank_deposits_private_sector_1963_70 (both 1963-1970 World Bank archival tables covering the same institutional balance-sheet concept) -- not numerically cross-checked against those in this pass (flagged as a natural follow-up QA step). Also includes national-accounts aggregates (gross_domestic_product, national_income, private_consumption_incl_stocks, government_consumption, gross_fixed_capital_formation, 1961-1971) -- these are fiscal/household-relevant top-line macro figures bundled in the same source table, kept in this one row rather than split out, since the source PDF genuinely presents them together as one country data page per issue. Source is IMF's own eLibrary Archived Series (free, no-login PDF downloads), transcribed via pdftotext + visual-render cross-check; NOT an Iranian-government primary source itself, but a foreign-multilateral report of Iran's own official statistics as submitted to the IMF for the era predating Iran's own CBI Annual Review series -- the closest available continuous coverage for 1937-1971 given that CBI's own historical time-series database (tsd.cbi.ir) is unreachable from this network (see download log).

title: Household Expenditure by Detailed Item, Urban & Rural, SCI Household Expenditure & Income Survey (SH1380-1399 / 2001-2020)
notes: Statistical Centre of Iran's own Household Expenditure & Income Survey (HEIS / آمارگیری هزینه و درآمد خانوار), the continuously-run survey the mission brief specifically named as target #2 -- extracted from Statistical Yearbook 1399 (2020/21) Chapter 21 by a prior session (2026-07-13, data/raw/sci-amar/household-expenditure-detail-2001-2020/) but never registered in CHART_REGISTRY.csv until this pass (confirmed via grep, zero prior hits -- a genuinely orphaned already-processed file). Covers total net expenditure plus food/non-food category breakdowns (including durable-goods/appliance and tea-coffee-cocoa line items) for both urban and rural areas. Marked 'extends' onto pahlavi_hh__expenditure_levels_rials_1965_1971 (Household Expenditure Levels, 1965-1971, from a 1974 World Bank report on the same underlying Iranian household-budget-survey tradition) because the raw file's own manifest documents this explicitly as 'the direct Islamic-Republic-era continuation' of that Pahlavi-era survey -- together the two give a bridge across the revolution for household consumption structure, though a genuine ~30-year gap (1971-2001) remains (the HEIS microdata that would fill it is registration-gated; see data/raw/iheis-microdata-metadata/access-instructions.md). Values are nominal thousand rials per household per year -- NOT yet deflated to real terms in this pass (heavily inflation-distorted across 2001-2020), flagged for a future currency-variant pass same as the raw file's own manifest already notes. HEIS data for 2021-2025 was searched for this pass and NOT found (amar.org.ir geo-blocked from this network on every attempt, direct and Wayback both -- see download log).

title: GDP by Final Expenditure Component incl. Household Consumption, Iran Data Portal (SH1370-1384 / 1991-2005)
notes: National-accounts-level household final consumption expenditure (plus private/NPISH/government final consumption and gross fixed capital formation), current-price billion rials, from an Iran Data Portal table mirroring an SCI/Plan & Budget Organization source -- downloaded 2026-07-12 but never harmonized/registered until this pass. Marked 'extends' onto iran_sci1399__gdp_by_expenditure_component_1390_1397 (the already-registered SCI Statistical Yearbook 1399 GDP-by-expenditure-component chart, SH1390-1397 / 2011-2018) because it is literally the same measure (GDP by final expenditure component) from an earlier period and a different specific source -- this row pushes the chart's real coverage back to 1991, filling in the decade before the already-registered chart's start. household_final_consumption_expenditure specifically is the direct national-accounts-aggregate counterpart to the HEIS household-budget-survey row above (different level of aggregation -- one is a survey of per-household averages, the other a macro aggregate -- not numerically cross-checked against each other in this pass, flagged as a natural follow-up).

title: Whole-Country Annual Budget Law Totals: Revenue/Resources vs. Expenditure/Uses (FY1371-1401)
notes: Whole-country revenue/expenditure totals read directly from the promulgated budget LAW TEXT itself (the single-article 'ماده واحده' clause) for 30 of 31 possible fiscal years FY1371-1401, including the internal Part A (general government) / Part A1 (general revenue) / Part A2 (earmarked revenue) / Part B (state companies+banks) breakdown where captured. Extracted by a prior session (2026-07-13) from 45 raw PDFs at data/raw/iran-plan-budget-org/ (Iran's own Plan & Budget Organization annual budget laws, via Iran Data Portal mirror since rc.majlis.ir itself is unreachable from this network) with a documented cross-validation method (spelled-out Persian number-words checked against parenthetical digit strings; part-sum reconciliation checked against printed grand totals) and already has real-USD/real-2015 computed variants -- but this chart_id was referenced BY NAME in another already-registered row's own notes field (iranplanbudgetorg__five_year_plan_investment_ceilings: '...same-era whole-country totals') without ever actually being registered itself, confirmed via grep before writing this row (1 hit, and it was that cross-reference, not a real registration). This is the single longest continuous PRIMARY-LEGAL-TEXT government-budget series in the project (30 years, 1992-2023) and squarely answers the mission's government-budget target. Complementary to (not merged with) iran_sci1399__government_budget_summary_1385_1400 (SCI Statistical Yearbook, FY1385-1400/2006-2021, a different source/table with a different resources/uses breakdown) and majlis__national_budget_totals_by_fiscal_year (earlier FY1341-1370/1963-1992 era) -- per this project's overlap policy, kept as separate rows rather than force-merged since they come from genuinely different source documents; a future pass could cross-validate the 2006-2021 overlap year-by-year between this chart and the SCI one, not done here.

title: Iran Monetary & Credit Aggregates, Quarterly Detail (M1/M2 Components, Bank Claims, Foreign Assets), 1998 Q4-2016 Q2
notes: 29 quarterly monetary/credit-aggregate metrics -- monetary base, currency-in-circulation, demand deposits, M1, quasi-money, M2, banking-system net foreign assets (split central-bank vs. banks), banking-system claims on public/private sector (split commercial vs. specialized banks), central-bank claims on government/public corporations, government/public-corp deposits with the banking system, notes-and-coins issued vs. held at banks, banks' legal/demand deposits at the central bank -- from Haver Analytics' Iran database, itself explicitly attributed column-by-column to 'Central Bank of the Islamic Republic of Iran' (.LSOURCE field), compiled/hosted by the Johns Hopkins University Institute for Applied Economics (Steve H. Hanke's institute; a legitimate academic re-host, not an excluded outlet per this project's source-reliability policy). Retrieved via the Wayback Machine since a direct curl to the live sites.krieger.jhu.edu URL returned HTTP 403. Marked 'extends' onto iran_monetary__liquidity_m2_monetary_base_2000_2023 (this pass's own new registration of the CBI Annual Review annual M2/monetary-base series) because it is the SAME primary underlying source (Central Bank of Iran) and largely the same 1998-2016 window, but at quarterly granularity with 29 metrics vs. the Annual Review's 2 -- a genuine depth addition, not a duplicate. **Independently cross-validated against the CBI Annual Review series during this pass**: 2001-Q1 M2 = 249,110.7 billion rials in this file EXACTLY matches cbi_annual_review_series/monetary_banking_aggregates_1379_1401.csv's FY1379 (2001) liquidity_m2_billion_rials = 249110.7 -- confirms both are genuinely the same CBI-sourced figure via two independent extraction routes, a real (not asserted) data-quality validation. Does NOT close the still-open 1972-1999 M1/M2 gap (starts 1998-Q4, only ~2 years earlier than the Annual Review's FY1379/2000-01 start) -- that gap remains a genuine, searched-for-and-not-found hole (see this pass's download log for the tsd.cbi.ir dead-end and other attempts).

title: Household Real Expenditure Growth, Poverty Rate & Gini Coefficient from SCI HEIS Microdata (SH1401-1403 / 2022-2025)
notes: Extends the SCI HEIS household-expenditure series (which otherwise stops at SH1399/2020 in this project) forward to SH1402-1403 (2023-2025) via Djavad Salehi-Isfahani's published analysis of SCI's own primary HEIS microdata (djavadsalehi.com, 'Tyranny of numbers' blog; Salehi-Isfahani is a named, credentialed economist, Professor at Virginia Tech, NOT on this project's source-exclusion list). amar.org.ir itself remains geo-blocked from this network for both direct and Wayback access on every attempt across this project (see download log) -- this is the best available route found this pass to post-2020 HEIS-derived figures. IMPORTANT CAVEAT (spelled out per this project's 'flag rather than silently treat as primary' convention): these are the author's OWN derived statistics computed from the raw microdata (regional real-per-capita-expenditure growth rates, a poverty rate against the author's own chosen poverty line since Iran has no official one, and a Gini coefficient) -- NOT verbatim SCI-published headline figures, and expressed as growth RATES / index values rather than the absolute thousand-rials expenditure LEVELS the 2001-2020 chart above uses, so this is a genuinely different (though related) metric family, kept in its own chart_id rather than force-merged row-for-row into the levels table. Two figures spot-checked directly against the raw saved HTML text before transcribing (poverty rate 17.5%->16.6%, cash transfer 9,000,000 rials/month) -- both confirmed present verbatim, not hallucinated by the fetch summarizer. Key figures: 2024 poverty rate 16.6% (down from 17.5% in 2023, ~1 million people lifted from poverty); 2024 real per-capita expenditure growth +0.9% rural / +3.4% other-urban / -2.4% Tehran-urban (a genuine regional divergence, not uniform growth); Gini coefficient rose slightly 0.397 (2022) to 0.409 (2023).
